title: Talking about guns in Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Something I always wondered...             Quote Post by EVA X » Mon May 31, 2010 10:49 am How come whenever the pallet rifles are fired, there is never any shell casings falling to the ground?

[]:             Quote Post by Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion » Mon May 31, 2010 10:58 am Maybe the shells stay inside of them until they're taken out?

[]:             Quote Post by EVA X » Mon May 31, 2010 11:02 am Why use extra space for casings instead of more bullets? Maybe the pallet rifle is as actually a large linear machine gun, so it doesn't need casings?

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Mon May 31, 2010 11:13 am Because the animation budget said so.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon May 31, 2010 12:13 pm I was going to say caseless rounds (fabricating shells that size would be ludicrously expensive), but now that I think about it, firing bullets that size would build up so much barrel heat that caseless ammo would be infeasible. Probably animation budget.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon May 31, 2010 12:46 pm Too big a pain in the ass to animate it, probably.

[]:             Quote Post by sephirotic » Mon May 31, 2010 1:04 pm BrikHaus wrote: Too big a pain in the ass to animate it, probably.

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Mon May 31, 2010 3:13 pm sephirotic wrote: though we do see shells in the rebuild 1.0 in others guns like the vulcan that unit 01 uses.

[]:             Quote Post by sephirotic » Mon May 31, 2010 4:49 pm ran1 wrote: NGE was working with a much smaller budget than Rebuild however, and also did not have the aid of any CG technology. 1995=/=2007

[]:             Quote Post by GasmaskAvenger » Mon May 31, 2010 5:02 pm they can have a similar design as the guns in the first Mass Effect had in terms of bullets

[]:             Quote Post by Halicat » Sat Jun 05, 2010 5:26 pm You know, I could have sworn they did at some points [I'll be damned if I know for certain though lol] if they didn't, it's probably the same reason they didn't animate the entry plug scenes properly: [Falling tears and static hair in LIQUID?] low budget. Granted, they didn't fix the LCL errors in rebuild so blame it on Anime physics/animator laziness/forgetfulness.

[]:             Quote Post by NatTheWriter » Sat Jun 05, 2010 6:03 pm Halicat wrote: Granted, they didn't fix the LCL errors in rebuild so blame it on Anime physics/animator laziness/forgetfulness.

[]:             Quote Post by Halicat » Sat Jun 05, 2010 6:13 pm NatTheWriter wrote: Eh, they were kind enough to add bubbles. :P Doesn't make it any less bizarre, of course.

[]:             Quote Post by NatTheWriter » Sat Jun 05, 2010 6:21 pm Halicat wrote: There is that I suppose XD And in the original series, they added slight voice distortion like talking through liquid so that was good, but since I haven't watched rebuild for a while, I can't recall them doing that in it?

[]:             Quote Post by Halicat » Sat Jun 05, 2010 6:23 pm NatTheWriter wrote: There was voice distortion? I recall it being used in the dub, but not in the original Japanese.

[]: Strange Gun from EP02             Quote Post by Orgeston » Thu Jan 28, 2016 3:12 am So, what the heck is this gun? Is it ever mentioned anywhere else?

[]: Re: Strange Gun from EP02             Quote Post by Orgeston » Thu Jan 28, 2016 4:15 am For that matter, what's the story with the positron rifle? How are the positron rifle and the positron sniper rifle two separate guns? They even both appear in EP22. The wiki lists the positron sniper rifle as the one requisitioned from the JSSDF's R&D labs, but you can clearly see the blue and white positron rifle as the one being taken from the lab. Did they take the positron rifle and completely engineer the positron sniper rifle from it in the duration of EP06?

[]: Re: Strange Gun from EP02             Quote Post by cyharding » Thu Jan 28, 2016 9:35 am It's been a long time since I've seen ep 6, but if I remember right, Ritsuko said to the effect that their own positron weapon was still in development and wouldn't be ready while the JSSDF's one, while more experimental, was workable, so it could be used.

[]: Re: Strange Gun from EP02             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 28, 2016 11:18 am Orgeston wrote: So, what the heck is this gun?

[]: Re: Strange Gun from EP02             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Thu Jan 28, 2016 2:48 pm It's the Yamashita sketch version of pallet rifle. The final pallet rifle design is much more animator-friendly. As for the Positron Rifles I'm assuming the designs are different because of what they wanted the firing pose to be. Operation Yashima needed prone, where the finalized rifle doesn't quite work. Arael you had the standing pose for 02 and the rifle looked badass as intended.
summary:
[]: Something I always wondered...             Quote Post by EVA X » Mon May 31, 2010 10:49 am How come whenever the pallet rifles are fired, there is never any shell casings falling to the ground?

[]:             Quote Post by Twin Drive Sigma Aquarion » Mon May 31, 2010 10:58 am Maybe the shells stay inside of them until they're taken out?

[]:       &nb
